title: Garlicky Aerated Egyptian Sherry Vinegar Tagine
description: A bold and aromatic tagine that combines the flavors of garlic, sherry vinegar, and Egyptian spices. A perfect dish for those seeking a unique culinary experience.
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine the sherry vinegar, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper. Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook until the mixture has reduced by half, about 10 minutes.

In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, heat a couple of tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat. Add the lamb shoulder and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the lamb back to the pot, along with the reduced vinegar mixture and salt and pepper to taste. Stir to combine, then cover the pot with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the tagine for about 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falls apart easily.
Meanwhile, grill the pine nuts in the robata grill until fragrant and lightly charred, about 5 minutes. Set aside for garnish.
To serve, place a portion of the tagine in each individual mini cocotte, then top with a sprinkle of grilled pine nuts and a dollop of yogurt (optional).
